title: Pure Core, UI Adapters, Dependency Direction, and Snapshots · Runtime Lens: Ui Ports
summary: Cells call Canvas methods, algorithms read widgets, tests require a display, and mutable state leaks across attempts. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ui ports increment in pure maze core with Tk adapter ports. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define narrow render, scheduling, input, status, dialog, persistence, and clock ports owned by the application layer.
Keep topology, generation, search, state transitions, and evidence free of Tk widget and operating-system dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-ui-ports — Define narrow render, scheduling, input, status, dialog, persistence, and clock ports owned by the application layer.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-architecture-pure-core-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-architecture-pure-core-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_architecture_pure_core_2(domain_modules: int = 7, ui_imports_in_core: int = 0, adapter_ports: int = 6, fake_adapter_cases: int = 18, snapshot_mutations: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if domain_modules < 1 or adapter_ports < 4:
        return False, adapter_ports, "architecture-shape"
    if ui_imports_in_core > 0:
        return False, ui_imports_in_core, "dependency-inversion"
    if fake_adapter_cases < 8:
        return False, fake_adapter_cases, "adapter-evidence"
    if snapshot_mutations > 0:
        return False, snapshot_mutations, "snapshot-isolation"
    assert ui_imports_in_core == 0 and snapshot_mutations == 0
    return True, fake_adapter_cases, "pure-core-boundary"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Ui Ports: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 2598 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce a pure domain core, narrow UI ports, immutable snapshots, and testable dependency direction; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.
